reasons: Estes can keep Miya healthy during extended front-to-back fights, giving her more time to convert attack-speed scaling into damage.
Miya benefits when Estes stays near the backline and supports her repeated basic-attack uptime.

proof.summary: Miya wants long attack uptime from a safe backline position, while Estes extends that window with repeated healing during grouped fights.
worksBestWhen: Miya stays behind the frontline within Estes' healing range.
The team plays grouped objective or front-to-back fights.
Miya has enough items to reward the sustained fight window.
failureCases: Enemy burst kills Miya before healing stabilizes her.
Miya positions too far from Estes during the fight.

reasons: Angela can attach to Miya with Heartguard and add a shield during the enemy dive window.
Miya can keep attacking while Angela supports her from the attached position.

proof.summary: Miya is fragile during late-game damage uptime, and Angela's Heartguard can shield and support her through a focused engage.
worksBestWhen: Angela saves Heartguard for the real dive or teamfight.
Miya is the main damage source worth protecting.
Miya continues spacing behind the frontline while shielded.
failureCases: Heartguard is on cooldown when the dive starts.
Enemy burst breaks through the shield quickly.
Miya is isolated beyond team peel after the shield ends.

reasons: Mathilda gives Miya shield value and a repositioning option through Guiding Wind.
Miya can use the added mobility to kite away from divers or follow safe cleanup opportunities.

proof.summary: Miya lacks a normal dash, while Mathilda can provide shield and movement support that helps Miya reposition during fights.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda stays close enough for Miya to use the mobility link.
Miya saves the reposition for enemy engage or cleanup.
The team plays around Miya as the protected backline carry.
failureCases: Guiding Wind is unavailable during the enemy engage.
Miya is rooted, suppressed, or burst before she can reposition.
Mathilda moves away from Miya before the link can be used.

reasons: Tigreal can start fights and keep enemies controlled while Miya attacks from the backline.
Grouped enemies increase the value of Miya's repeated basic attacks and teamfight damage window.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates a grouped crowd-control opening, and Miya follows from safe range with sustained basic-attack damage.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages within Miya's follow-up range.
Miya waits behind the frontline before attacking.
The team is ready to protect Miya after Tigreal commits.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far from Miya.
Enemy assassins bypass the frontline and reach Miya.
Enemies spread before Tigreal creates a grouped target.

reasons: Diggie can protect Miya from crowd-control chains that would stop her attack uptime.
Time Journey supports Miya during major teamfights by giving nearby protection during the enemy engage window.

proof.summary: Miya needs uninterrupted attack uptime, while Diggie can protect nearby teammates during a crowd-control engage with Time Journey.
worksBestWhen: Diggie stays close enough to cover Miya.
Diggie saves Time Journey for the enemy's real engage.
Miya keeps attacking after the protection window starts.
failureCases: Diggie uses Time Journey before the main engage.
Miya is caught outside Diggie's protection range.
Enemy waits out the protection window before committing damage.
